description:
You are a QA engineer who must perform a very quick security-keyword sweep of some demo application source code that already exists on disk.

    1. A tiny project is located in “/home/user/projects/app”.  
       The agent must not move or rename any of its files.

    2. Create the directory “/home/user/scan_report” (if it does not already exist) and write a single log file called  
       “/home/user/scan_report/security_report.log”.

    3. Scan *only* the files that are inside “/home/user/projects/app” (no sub-directories exist other than “app” itself).  
       Search case-insensitively for the three keywords listed below:  
          • password  
          • secret  
          • TODO  

    4. For every line in which one of those words appears, write one line to the log that follows **exactly** this format (no spaces outside those shown, no extra punctuation):

       <relative_path_from_/home/user/projects>:<line_number>:<matched_keyword_in_lowercase>:<line_text_without_leading_whitespace>

       • “relative_path_from_/home/user/projects” means that the prefix “/home/user/projects/” must be stripped off.  
       • “line_number” is the 1-based line in the file.  
       • “matched_keyword_in_lowercase” must be written in lowercase even if the original text was uppercase.  
       • “line_text_without_leading_whitespace” is the full source line as it appeared in the file but left-trimmed so it does not begin with any spaces or tab characters. Do **not** right-trim; keep any trailing spaces the original line might have (there are none in the sample files).

    5. After you have written one entry per finding, append one final summary line that is *exactly*:

       TOTAL_FINDINGS:<number>

       where “<number>” is the count of previous finding lines in the file.

    Example of the required log structure (the actual lines and counts will depend on the scan results):

       app/somefile.py:12:todo:# TODO: refactor this  
       app/api.py:45:password:password = "hunter2"  
       TOTAL_FINDINGS:2

    6. Overwrite “security_report.log” if it already exists, then output (e.g. with “cat”) its full contents to the terminal so the CI system can capture it.

    Hints (do not reveal exact commands):
       • You will likely accomplish everything with 2–3 shell commands using a recursive search tool such as grep, followed by a redirect.  
       • Remember to run the search from the base directory “/home/user/projects” so that the paths written to the log are relative as required.

    The automated grader will:
       • Check that “/home/user/scan_report/security_report.log” exists.  
       • Verify its contents follow the strict format described above and that the summary count matches the number of finding lines.  
       • Confirm you displayed the file contents to the console.

    Do not include commentary in the log file itself—only the lines described above.
truth:
PRE-EXISTING FILES (before the agent starts):

    1. /home/user/projects/app/main.py  
       Line 1: # Sample app  
       Line 2: def login():  
       Line 3:     # TODO: improve security  
       Line 4:     password = "12345"  

    2. /home/user/projects/app/utils.py  
       Line 1: # utility  
       Line 2: secret_key = "abcde"  
       Line 3: def helper():  
       Line 4:     pass  

    EXPECTED CONTENTS OF /home/user/scan_report/security_report.log **after** the agent completes the task:

    app/main.py:3:todo:# TODO: improve security
    app/main.py:4:password:password = "12345"
    app/utils.py:2:secret:secret_key = "abcde"
    TOTAL_FINDINGS:3
name: task_000000_64c0ee72
